steps.title: Identify the slope of line k
steps.content: From the image, identify that line \(k\)'s slope is \(-\frac{3}{4}\). Since line \(j\) is perpendicular to line \(k\), its slope will be the negative reciprocal. Therefore, the slope of line \(j\) is \(m = \frac{4}{3}\).

steps.title: Write the equation for line j using point-slope form
steps.content:
Since we know the slope of line \(j\) is \(\frac{4}{3}\) and it passes through the point \((-20, -23)\), use the point-slope equation
  • $y - y_1 = m(x - x_1)$
. Substitute the slope and the point's coordinates:
$$y + 23 = \frac{4}{3}(x + 20)\quad.\quad \text{Simplifying this gives us } y = \frac{4}{3}x - 38.$$

steps.title: Match the equation to the choices
steps.content:
The simplified form of line \(j\)'s equation, $y = \frac{4}{3}x - 38$, matches choice D.
That matches choice D — D is correct.
